seoTitle: How to Assign Multiple Assignees in Jira
metaDescription: Jira issues natively support only one assignee, so here's how teams work around that to track multiple people on the same piece of work.
intro: Jira's standard assignee field only supports one person per issue, which can be limiting when multiple people are actually working on the same ticket. This guide covers the common workarounds teams use to track shared ownership.

steps.heading: Understand the single-assignee limitation
steps.body: Jira issues have one assignee field by design, so tracking multiple contributors requires a workaround rather than a built-in multi-select field.

steps.heading: Use a custom multi-user field
steps.body: Create a custom field with a multi-user picker type, then add it to your issue screen so you can list additional contributors alongside the primary assignee.

steps.heading: Split work into subtasks
steps.body: Alternatively, break the issue into subtasks and assign each subtask to a different person, keeping the parent issue as the shared container.

steps.heading: Use a label or comment convention
steps.body: Some teams simply tag additional contributors in a comment or with a label as a lightweight way to note who else is involved.

steps.heading: Choose the approach that fits your workflow
steps.body: Decide between a custom field, subtasks, or a lightweight convention based on how precisely your team needs to track shared ownership.

faq.question: Does Jira support multiple assignees out of the box?
faq.answer: No, the standard assignee field only allows one person, so tracking multiple contributors requires a custom field or another workaround.

faq.question: What's the most common way teams handle this?
faq.answer: Many teams either add a custom multi-user field to track additional contributors or split the work into subtasks assigned to different people.

faq.question: Are there marketplace apps that add multi-assignee support?
faq.answer: Yes, several Jira marketplace apps are built specifically to add native-feeling multiple assignee functionality if the built-in workarounds aren't enough.

faq.question: Does using subtasks for multiple people affect reporting?
faq.answer: It can, since work gets split at the subtask level, so your reports may need to account for subtasks rather than just the parent issue.
